title: QuietLift
content:
{{Top|ca}}
{{Youmay|the [[canon]] manufacturing company|the [[QuietLift 1400|repulsorlift engine]] in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity|linebreak=1}}

The manufacturing company '''QuietLift''' produced [[repulsorlift]] [[engine]]s that did not produce the shimmer between a vehicle using the engine and the [[planet]]ary surface it was pushing against, which was produced by some short-range repulsorlifts.<ref name="BMF 38" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the current ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]], QuietLift was mentioned in the article "Repulsorlifts," which was published in the "[[Secrets of Spaceflight]]" department of the [[Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon 38|thirty-eighth issue]] of [[De Agostini]]'s ''[[Star Wars: Build the Millennium Falcon]]'' magazine<ref name="BMF 38" /> around [[September 23]], [[2015]].<ref name="BMFdate">{{BMFdates|38}}</ref> In the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, the [[QuietLift 1400]] was a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] [[Engine/Legends|engine]] that was first mentioned in ''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]'', a [[1987]] supplement for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' written by [[Bill Slavicsek]] and [[Curtis Smith]] and published by [[West End Games]].<ref name="SWS">''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]''</ref>
